It is a platform to search, visualize, and download nucleomics data.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.CugtbQ","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.CugtbQ","homepage":"https://data.4dnucleome.org/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1855,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"The European Searchable Tumour Line Database","abbreviation":"IPD-ESTDAB","description":"The European Searchable Tumour Line Database (ESTDAB) Database and Cell Bank provide a service enabling investigators to search online for HLA typed, immunologically characterised tumour cells.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.pypsym","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.pypsym","homepage":"https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ipd/estdab/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1607,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"MINAS - A Database of Metal Ions in Nucleic AcidS","abbreviation":"MINAS","description":"MINAS contains the exact geometric information on the first and second-shell coordinating ligands of every metal ion present in nucleic acid structures that are deposited in the PDB and NDB. Containing also the sequence information of the binding pocket-proximal nucleotides, this database allows for a detailed search of all combinations of potential ligands and of coordination environments of metal ions. MINAS is therefore a perfect new tool to classify metal ion binding pockets in nucleic acids by statistics and to draw general conclusions about the different coordination properties of these ions. This record has been marked as Uncertain because the homepage for this resource is no longer active, and we have not been able to get in touch with the owners of the resource. Please contact us if you have any information regarding MINAS.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.wqtfkv","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.wqtfkv","homepage":"http://www.minas.uzh.ch","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1681,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"neXtProt","abbreviation":"neXtProt","description":"neXtProt is a comprehensive human-centric discovery platform, offering its users a seamless integration of and navigation through protein-related data.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.62evqh","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.62evqh","homepage":"https://www.expasy.org/archives/nextprot","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1760,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase_and_repository","name":"PHI-base","abbreviation":null,"description":"PHI-base database contains expertly curated molecular and biological information on genes proven to affect the outcome of pathogen-host interactions. PHI-base catalogues experimentally verified pathogenicity, virulence and effector genes from fungal, Oomycete and bacterial pathogens, which infect animal, plant, fungal and insect hosts. An online community  curation portal, called PHI-Canto (https://canto.phi-base.org) supports article curation by authors with datasets being displayed at http://phi5.phi-base.org.\nData and source code availability: All data sets are available from Github at  https://github.com/PHI-base. \nDiscussion board: curation issues for community curation can be discussed and followed using the PHI-canto curation tracker (https://github.com/PHI-base/curation)\nFunding: PHI-base is funded from the UK Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council (BBSRC) [BB/S020020/1]. \nCitation reference: Urban, M. et al (2022). NAR doi: 10.1093/nar/gkab1037","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.73cqdk","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.73cqdk","homepage":"http://www.phi-base.org/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1867,"registry":"database","record_type":"repository","name":"Ensembl","abbreviation":null,"description":"Ensembl creates, integrates and distributes reference datasets and analysis tools that enable genomics. Ensembl is a genome browser that supports research in comparative genomics, evolution, sequence variation and transcriptional regulation. Ensembl annotate genes, computes multiple alignments, predicts regulatory function and collects disease data.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.fx0mw7","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.fx0mw7","homepage":"http://www.ensembl.org/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1901,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"KinMutBase","abbreviation":"KinMutBase","description":"KinMutBase is a comprehensive database of disease-causing mutations in protein kinase domains. This resources provides plenty of information, namely mutation statistics and display, clickable sequences with mutations and changes to restriction enzyme patterns.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.q2ntvx","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.q2ntvx","homepage":"http://structure.bmc.lu.se/idbase/KinMutBase/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1895,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"BAliBASE","abbreviation":"BAliBASE","description":"BAliBASE; a benchmark alignment database, including enhancements for repeats, transmembrane sequences and circular permutations.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.sed5tq","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.sed5tq","homepage":"http://www.lbgi.fr/balibase/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":5928,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase_and_repository","name":"PharmFreq","abbreviation":"PharmFreq","description":"Pharmacogenetic (PGx) variability in drug metabolism and transport significantly influences individual differences in drug efficacy and safety. Genetic variants, particularly within the CYP, UGT, and SULT families, account for much of the variability in enzyme and transporter expression and activity. While PGx guidelines provide evidence-based recommendations for drug selection and dosing in personalized medicine, effective global implementation requires access to population-specific PGx variant frequencies.\n\nThe Pharmacogenetic Allele Frequency (PharmFreq) database serves as a comprehensive knowledgebase, repository, and search tool for country-specific pharmacogenetic allele frequencies on a global scale. It allows users to explore the ethnogeographic variability of genetically encoded drug response differences and to identify outlier populations that may benefit from tailored therapeutic adjustments. By facilitating an easy access to this information, PharmFreq supports the broader clinical integration of pharmacogenomics.","doi":null,"url":"https://fairsharing.org/5928","homepage":"https://www.pharmfreq.com/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":2612,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"Database of Kashiwa Encyclopedia for human genome mutations in Regulatory regions and their Omics contexts","abbreviation":"DBKERO","description":"The Database of Kashiwa Encyclopedia for human genome mutations in Regulatory regions and their Omics contexts (DBKERO) stores transcriptome information with a catalogue of genomic variations including public SNP data and epigenome information to enable further in-depth analyses on the disease-causing molecular mechanisms. Recent additions include genomic variation datasets and epigenome variation datasets focusing on the Japanese population. These datasets were collected as a series of genome-wide association studies and as part of International Human Epigenome Consortium (IHEC) projects. The goal is to have this database serve as a model case for the genomic, epigenomic and transcriptomic variations occurring in particular ethnic backgrounds and underlying various diseases. Clinical samples data were also associated with data from various model systems such as drug perturbation datasets using cultured cancer cells.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.3fa02d","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.3fa02d","homepage":"http://kero.hgc.jp/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":2530,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"Alliance of Genome Resources","abbreviation":null,"description":"The primary mission of the Alliance of Genome Resources (the Alliance) is to develop and maintain sustainable genome information resources that facilitate the use of diverse model organisms in understanding the genetic and genomic basis of human biology, health and disease.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.6ba5fw","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.6ba5fw","homepage":"https://www.alliancegenome.org","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":2572,"registry":"database","record_type":"repository","name":"Pulse Crop Database","abbreviation":"PCD","description":"Although there is wealth of legume genetic research information in North America and throughout the world, there is still a need for a system that can organize, filter and provide analysis on the available research to be directly applied in breeding programs. The Pulse Crop Database (formerly the Cool Season Food Legume Crop Database) is being developed to serve as a resource for Genomics-Assisted Breeding (GAB). GAB offers tools to identify genes related to traits of interest among other methods to optimize plant breeding efficiency and research, by providing relevant genomic, genetic and breeding information and analysis.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.8d1f51","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.8d1f51","homepage":"https://www.pulsedb.org/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":2577,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"Genome Database for Vaccinium","abbreviation":"GDV","description":"The amount of genetic research data for Vaccinium is steadily increasing and there is a need for a system that can organize, filter and provide analysis of the available research to be directly applied in breeding programs. The Genome Database for Vaccinium (GDV) is a curated and integrated web-based relational database. The GDV integrates genomic, genetic and breeding data for blueberry, cranberry and other Vaccinium species. The GDV includes Vaccinium genomes, annotated transcripts, traits, maps and markers.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.221d46","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.221d46","homepage":"https://www.vaccinium.org/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":2570,"registry":"database","record_type":"repository","name":"Citrus Genome Database","abbreviation":"CGD","description":"The Citrus Genome Database, known as CGD, is a USDA and NSF funded resource to enable basic, translational and applied research in citrus. It houses genomics, genetics and breeding data for citrus species and organisms associated with HLB.  It is built by the Mainlab at Washington State University using the open-source, generic database platform Tripal.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.a04fb2","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.a04fb2","homepage":"https://www.citrusgenomedb.org/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":2816,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase_and_repository","name":"The database of eukaryotic RNA binding proteins","abbreviation":"EuRBPDB","description":"EuRBPDB is a comprehensive and user-friendly database for classifying and annotating eukaryotic RNA binding proteins (RBPs) drawn from various public databases. Over 100 eukaryotic species such as human, mouse, fly, worm and yeast are included. EuRBPDB also provides information on known and potential cancer-associated RBPs.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.fr95Qm","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.fr95Qm","homepage":"http://eurbpdb.gzsys.org.cn/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1563,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"ConoServer","abbreviation":"ConoServer","description":"ConoServer is a database specializing in sequences and structures of peptides expressed by marine cone snails. The database gives access to protein sequences, nucleic acid sequences and structural information on conopeptides. ConoServer's data are first collected from the peer reviewed literature and from publicly available databases, including UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot, NCBI nucleotide (nt), and the World Wide Protein Data Bank. The data are then curated manually, which include the addition of references, the analysis of sequence regions, the identification of cysteine frameworks and gene superfamilies, and the identification of mature peptides in the precusor sequences.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.y00hz4","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.y00hz4","homepage":"http://www.conoserver.org","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1646,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"SNPeffect","abbreviation":"SNPeffect","description":"SNPeffect is a database for phenotyping human single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs). SNPeffect primarily focuses on the molecular characterization and annotation of disease and polymorphism variants in the human proteome. Further, SNPeffect holds per-variant annotations on functional sites, structural features and post-translational modification.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.qbt1gh","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.qbt1gh","homepage":"http://snpeffect.switchlab.org","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1832,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase_and_repository","name":"Information system for G protein-coupled receptors","abbreviation":"GPCRdb","description":"The GPCRDB is a molecular-class information system that collects, combines, validates and stores large amounts of heterogenous data on G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs). The GPCRDB contains data on sequences, ligand binding constants and mutations. In addition, many different types of computationally derived data are stored such as multiple sequence alignments and homology models.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.e4n3an","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.e4n3an","homepage":"https://gpcrdb.org/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1852,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"Gene Ontology Annotation Database","abbreviation":"GOA","description":"The GO Annotation Database (GOA) provides Gene Ontology (GO) annotations to proteins in the UniProt Knowledgebase (UniProtKB), RNA molecules from RNACentral and protein complexes from the Complex Portal. GOA files contain a mixture of manual annotation supplied by members of the Gene Onotology Consortium and computationally assigned GO terms describing gene products. Annotation type is clearly indicated by associated evidence codes and there are links to the source data.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.7zffgc","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.7zffgc","homepage":"http://www.ebi.ac.uk/GOA","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1951,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase_and_repository","name":"Rat Genome Database","abbreviation":"RGD","description":"The Rat Genome Database stores genetic, genomic, phenotype, and disease data generated from rat research. It provides access to corresponding data for nine other species, allowing cross-species comparison. Data curation is performed both manually and via an automated pipeline, giving RGD users integrated access to a wide variety of data to support their research.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.pfg82t","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.pfg82t","homepage":"https://rgd.mcw.edu/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":4329,"registry":"database","record_type":"repository","name":"ERIC/open","abbreviation":"ERIC-open","description":"The Eawag Research Data Institutional Collection (ERIC) is a CKAN based data repository for the sharing of open research data produced by eawag scientists. \nEawag, The Swiss Federal Institute of Aquatic Science and Technology, is concerned with concepts and technologies for dealing sustainably with water bodies and with water as a resource. Our repository metadata conforms to Datacite (v4) metadata standards","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.1a0bbf","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.1a0bbf","homepage":"https://opendata.eawag.ch/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":2859,"registry":"database","record_type":"knowledgebase","name":"Cyclebase","abbreviation":"Cyclebase","description":"Cyclebase is a database to visualize and download results from genome-wide cell-cycle-related experiments. In addition to genome annotation, Cyclebase also provides mRNA data, protein expression data, and integrated cell-cycle phenotype information from high-content screens and model organism databases.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.dOFQ7p","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.dOFQ7p","homepage":"https://cyclebase.org/CyclebaseSearch","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""}],"links":{"self":null,"first":null,"prev":null,"next":null,"last":null},"note":"Proxied for use in Data Stewardship Wizard (see https://ds-wizard.org for more)"}

response.body: {"data":[{"id":1245,"registry":"standard","record_type":"terminology_artefact","name":"Dublin Core Metadata Element Set","abbreviation":"DCES","description":"The Dublin Metadata Element Set, which is often called Dublin Core (DC), is a standardized metadata scheme for description of any kind of resource such as documents in electronic and non-electronic form, digital materials (such as video, sound, images, etc) and composite media like web pages. Dublin Core Metadata may be used for multiple purposes, from simple resource description, to combining metadata vocabularies of different metadata standards, to providing interoperability for metadata vocabularies in the Linked Data cloud and Semantic Web implementations. Please note that this version of the specification for the Dublin Core Element Set 1.1 is somewhat out of date, although it is not officially deprecated. The DCMI Metadata Terms specification is linked to this record and is the current documentation that should be used for the Dublin Core Element Set 1.1. This document, an excerpt from the more comprehensive document \"DCMI Metadata Terms\" [DCTERMS] provides an abbreviated reference version of the fifteen element descriptions that have been formally endorsed in the following standards: ISO Standard 15836:2009 of February 2009 [ISO15836], ANSI/NISO Standard Z39.85-2012 of February 2013 [NISOZ3985], and IETF RFC 5013 of August 2007 [RFC5013].","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.3nx7t","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.3nx7t","homepage":"https://www.dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/dces/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":725,"registry":"standard","record_type":"model_and_format","name":"Corpus Encoding Standards","abbreviation":"CES / XCES","description":"CES provides a unit coding standard for linguistic corpus annotation. The CES can be used to encode corpora as resources for natural language processing. XCES is the XML version.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.5d5322","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.5d5322","homepage":"https://www.cs.vassar.edu/CES/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":4857,"registry":"standard","record_type":"terminology_artefact","name":"Clinical MetaData Ontology","abbreviation":"CMDO","description":"The Clinical MetaData Ontology is designed to be used to classify Common Data Elements (CDEs) from different studies and clinical documents.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.eb12a9","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.eb12a9","homepage":"https://bioportal.bioontology.org/ontologies/CMDO","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":4688,"registry":"standard","record_type":"model_and_format","name":"PICA XML","abbreviation":"pica-XML","description":"PICA XML is a standard to encode PICA+ in XML (in short: pica-XML) for working with PICA+ data in XML environments. PICA+ XML allows transformations between PICA+ and other metadata formats, as well as presentation, validation, analysis, and editing of PICA+ data with XML-based tools.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.9a67d2","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.9a67d2","homepage":"https://verbundwiki.gbv.de/display/VZG/PICA+XML+Version+1.0","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1477,"registry":"standard","record_type":"identifier_schema","name":"IUPAC International Chemical Identifier","abbreviation":"InChI","description":"Originally developed by the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC), the IUPAC International Chemical Identifier (InChI) is a machine-readable string generated from a chemical structure. InChIs are unique to the compound they describe and can encode absolute stereochemistry making chemicals and chemistry machine-readable and discoverable. A simple analogy is that InChI is the bar-code for chemistry and chemical structures. A hashed fixed-length version of the InChI, the InChIKey, is also widely used.  The InChI format and algorithm are non-proprietary and the software is open source, with ongoing development done by the community. A number of IUPAC working groups are working on extensions and new applications of the standard.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.ddk9t9","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.ddk9t9","homepage":"https://www.inchi-trust.org/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":912,"registry":"standard","record_type":"model_and_format","name":"JavaScript Object Notation for Simple Knowledge Organization Systems","abbreviation":"JSKOS","description":"JSKOS (JavaScript Object Notation for Simple Knowledge Organization Systems) defines a JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) structure to encode knowledge organization systems (KOS), such as classifications, thesauri, and authority files. JSKOS supports encoding of concepts, concept schemes, concept occurrences, and concept mappings with their common properties. It further defines application profiles for registries, distributions, and annotations. The main part of JSKOS is compatible with Simple Knowledge Organisation System (SKOS) and JavaScript Object Notation for Linked Data (JSON-LD) but JSKOS can be used without having to be experienced in any of these technologies. A simple JSKOS document can be mapped to SKOS expressed in the Resource Description Framework (RDF), and vice versa. JSKOS further supports closed world statements to express incomplete information about knowledge organization systems to facilitate use in dynamic web applications.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.81f1d3","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.81f1d3","homepage":"http://gbv.github.io/jskos/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":1255,"registry":"standard","record_type":"terminology_artefact","name":"Menelas Project Top-Level Ontology","abbreviation":"TOP-MENELAS","description":"The two main goals MENELAS contributes to are to (i) Provide better account of and better access to medical information through natural languages in order to help physicians in their daily practice, and to (ii) Enhance European cooperation by multilingual access to standardised medical nomenclatures. The major achievements of MENELAS are the realisation of its two functional systems: (i) The Document Indexing System encodes free text PDSs into both an internal representation (a set of Conceptual Graphs) and international nomenclature codes (ICD-9-CM). Instances of the Document Indexing System have been realised for French, English and Dutch ; (ii) The Consultation System allows users to access the information contained in PDSs previously indexed by the Document Indexing System. The test domain for the project was coronary diseases. The existing prototype shows promising results for information retrieval from natural language PDSs and for automatically encoding PDSs into an existing classification such as ICD-9-CM. A set of components, tools, knowledge bases and methods has also been produced by the project. These include language-independent ontology and models for the domain of coronary diseases; conceptual description of the relevant ICD-9-CM codes. This ontology includes a top-ontology, a top-domain ontology and a domain ontology (Coronay diseases surgery). The menelas-top ontology here is the part of the whole ontology without any reference to medical domain.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.j2np9m","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.j2np9m","homepage":"https://bioportal.lirmm.fr/ontologies/TOP-MENELAS","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":537,"registry":"standard","record_type":"terminology_artefact","name":"Ontology of the amphioxus anatomy and life cycle","abbreviation":"AMPHX","description":"The ontology of the amphioxus anatomy and life cycle is the first ontology for the development and anatomy of Amphioxus (Branchiostoma lanceolatum).","doi":null,"url":"https://fairsharing.org/537","homepage":"https://github.com/EBISPOT/amphx_ontology","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":7586,"registry":"standard","record_type":"terminology_artefact","name":"SI Reference Point","abbreviation":"SIRP","description":"Authoritative digital reference for the International System of Units (SI). It provides semantic models and persistent digital identifiers for the named SI units, SI prefixes, and defining constants.","doi":null,"url":"https://fairsharing.org/7586","homepage":"https://si-digital-framework.org/SI","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":5137,"registry":"standard","record_type":"model_and_format","name":"openMINDS metadata framework","abbreviation":"openMINDS","description":"The openMINDS metadata framework comprises: schema specifications for multiple, interlocked metadata models for Linked Data, instance libraries for serviceable, well defined terms and constructs, as well as supportive tooling. openMINDS is developed as open source project by the Open Metadata Initiative on GitHub. The framework is distributed over multiple, interlinked GitHub projects with one main GitHub repository for central access and maintenance.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.6ac6aa","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.6ac6aa","homepage":"https://github.com/openMetadataInitiative/openMINDS","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":5143,"registry":"standard","record_type":"model_and_format","name":"Standard communications protocol for computer assisted electrocardiography","abbreviation":"SCP-ECG V3.0","description":"Standard communications protocol for computer assisted electrocardiography V 3.0 . It is also defined in the joint ANSI/AAMI standard EC71:2001 and in the CEN standard EN 1064:2005.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.3cd777","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.3cd777","homepage":"https://www.iso.org/standard/84664.html","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""},{"id":6552,"registry":"standard","record_type":"terminology_artefact","name":"Paleoclimatology (thesaurus)","abbreviation":null,"description":"The result of a collaboration between paleoclimatologists and scientific information experts, the thesaurus of paleoclimatology describes and structures for the first time about 2000 terms and concepts of the discipline, in French and in English.","doi":"10.25504/FAIRsharing.53613a","url":"https://doi.org/10.25504/FAIRsharing.53613a","homepage":"https://loterre.istex.fr/QX8/en/","countries":[],"disciplines":[],"domains":[],"taxonomies":[],"user_defined_tags":[],"legacy_ids":[],"created_at":"","updated_at":""}],"links":{"self":null,"first":null,"prev":null,"next":null,"last":null},"note":"Proxied for use in Data Stewardship Wizard (see https://ds-wizard.org for more)"}

1796fa3c-9f53-475f-89ff-c66a0453c42e.description: In many research projects, the finished data management plan is one of the early deliverables. Questions that should be addressed in those early months of the project are highlighted as desirable when this phase is selected. Regular updates should be conducted as the project develops.
1796fa3c-9f53-475f-89ff-c66a0453c42e.title: Initial DMP

adc9133d-afcd-4616-9aea-db5f475898a2.description: When a project rounds up, this means that all data has found its final spots. In this phase, questions that document the final state of affairs will be labeled as desirable.
adc9133d-afcd-4616-9aea-db5f475898a2.title: Complete DMP (concluding the project)

b5a77002-ba95-4752-9cb9-c0f232d84379.description: Regular updates of the DMP should be conducted as the project develops. Questions that should be (re-)adressed while revising the DMP project are highlighted as desirable when this phase is selected.
b5a77002-ba95-4752-9cb9-c0f232d84379.title: Midway DMP

009b3738-7afd-47c1-b685-ab7bd2ff2568.text: Briefly describe which metadata and documentation requirements the archive has.
009b3738-7afd-47c1-b685-ab7bd2ff2568.title: Is it clear which metadata standard(s) will be used?

019db0b3-9067-4134-8bfd-76db3cfc572a.title: Are there privacy reasons why your data cannot be open?

024bb661-70d4-4da6-a938-934a3935b16a.title: Describe the structure of the data

0259866a-50b2-4f77-858f-982c9e16091d.title: Is it clear what a row in the table represents?

02b3fed1-0b50-4a80-b8b6-a225a1107022.text: It is important to ensure that the data in your project is not corrupted e.g. through transfer problems, data degradation (bitrot), vandalism, or human error. This is particularly important if data is transferred between sites/machines, and in complex projects with several people involved.
02b3fed1-0b50-4a80-b8b6-a225a1107022.title: Will you monitor data integrity once it has been collected?

03a8ff78-27e4-4852-94be-3668b3feb772.title: Name of the policy or guideline

0451d421-4c97-452e-b399-4fe278b97147.text: It may appear that in a table with 'patients' as rows, a column labeled 'disease' coupled to an ontology has a clear meaning. But that is not always explicit enough! A 'disease' could e.g. be the disease that the patient is suffering from, but it could also be an earlier diagnose, a suspected diagnose, or the disease a family member recently died of.
0451d421-4c97-452e-b399-4fe278b97147.title: Is the relation between each of the columns and the record identifier clear?

0490b04d-f066-40a9-8c52-be6e004f12b7.text: Provide a link to the dataset/record/source, preferably referring to its persistent identifier.
0490b04d-f066-40a9-8c52-be6e004f12b7.title: Link/reference to the data/record/source

04d55fac-ceb4-489a-bce2-049793ae32a2.text:
Briefly explain why you are deleting data(sets).

Examples:
* Legal obligations for health data
* Contractual obligations
* Raw data that never needs to be reprocessed
* Intermediate data that is easily reproduced
* There is no value, nobody will ever reuse it (e.g. data from technically failed experiments)
04d55fac-ceb4-489a-bce2-049793ae32a2.title: Reasons for deletion

04e2375b-b5f5-47dc-939b-2599ee1af0ba.text: Example: DMP of connected project, v1.0.0, accessible at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.1234567
04e2375b-b5f5-47dc-939b-2599ee1af0ba.title: Related DMP(s)

0551adc9-7faa-4ddc-a07b-08fbde2a247b.text: Data are likely to be moved and copied, and over time people may lose track of their origin. It can therefore be helpful to store licences (preferably as open as possible) in close association with the data.
0551adc9-7faa-4ddc-a07b-08fbde2a247b.title: Will you store the licenses with the data at all time?

06655161-9a91-47f4-af3b-9d2a5e6db19b.title: How is the intellectual property for the data collected in the project otherwise arranged?

067aab0b-c02b-49fc-b93a-3e109a5b6f04.title: Is the software registered in a domain-specific registry?

07d8e4e1-e85e-4e27-8711-8a97366f7574.text:
Data processing may affect information security classification.

Example: Anonymisation of personal data
07d8e4e1-e85e-4e27-8711-8a97366f7574.title: Will data processing affect information security?

09b7c406-bf87-4a28-87cb-cb962308c174.title: Is there a documented way to report bugs and other usability issues?

Which data format will be collected? Is the file format standard and widely used by researchers in this field? 

Does this data format enable sharing and long term archiving? If file format conversions will be necessary, remember to describe them in the chapter 'Processing, analysing and interpreting data'.

Examples:
* Tabular data (file format: .csv)
* Interview transcripts as text files (file format: .txt)

0e3ae229-e343-4e80-b9fa-8749e6db5ae4.text:
It can be worthwhile to consider which data in a project only have intermediate value and which data should be preserved long-term. This is particularly important if storage of large amounts of data is associated with costs. Early archiving of data in public repositories (with open or restricted access as appropriate) is recommended.

If applicable, describe your overarching strategy for selecting datasets for preservation or deletion.

If you are combining different types of data or data from different sources it is likely that you will need to match items and glue everything together. This can be done with traditional table database technology, but it is also possible to use Linked Data and RDF.

You may also need to use "mappings" to match data together.

1a015107-e2c3-42ee-b78c-4daf9286c56d.text:
When possible, please add all "formal" identifiers you have for this data set: these can be handles or DOIs or any other type. One important purpose of these identifiers is to be able to find the data set.

A good identifier is _persistent_ (i.e. it does not change, and also the same identifier will never be used for another data set), _globally unique_ (nobody else uses the same identifier for a different data set) and _resolvable_ (you can actually locate the data set if you only know the identifier).

Note that if you will be publishing the dataset in a repository, the repository may also assign a persistent identifier. You will be able to specify such additional identifiers under the "publication"-questions. Specify one main ident

2a451fdb-e579-4050-9b80-e9fd40ce8c92.text:
List all storage and computing solutions that you are planning to use in the course of the project. Consider all digital data that you are expecting to handle in the course of the project, including files only kept temporary (e.g. intermediate computational/analysis results) and working documents (e.g. manuscripts, electronic lab notebooks).

Examples:
* Local hardware
* Institutionally managed cloud storage (e.g. OneDrive, Educloud operated by UiO)
* Institutionally managed data servers or network drives
* Institutionally managed cold storage (tape storage)
* Trusted research environments (TSD provided by UiO, HUNT cloud provided by NTNU, SAFE provided by UiB)
* National storage infrastructure (e.g. NIRD provided by Sigma2)
* HPC infrastructure (e.g. NREC operated by UiB and UiO)
* Other cloud storage (e.g. Google Drive, Dropbox)
* Online version control services (e.g. GitHub, GitLab)
* Online document services (e.g. Overleaf, Googledocs)
* Online platforms offering software-as-a-service
2a451fdb-e579-4050-9b80-e9fd40ce8c92.title: Where will you store data and documents?

43e2efe4-016c-4a20-8b94-526ae1799afa.text:
Studies can rely solely on pre-existing sources or data, combine data reuse with generating novel data, or discard the idea of reusing data.

Many projects make use of datasets published in discipline-specific, institutional and generic research-data archives, registry data, or so-called "reference data" which is used by many projects. 

As many of the same considerations apply, also access to materials that are commonly referred to as 'records' or 'sources' rather than 'data' should be included here, such as material in public archives, media archives, legal resources, or large amounts of digital literature.

Examples:
* Reference datasets in molecular biology
* Published dataset in generic research data archive
* Digitalized historical record in digital archive
* Record in public database
43e2efe4-016c-4a20-8b94-526ae1799afa.title: The project will (re)use datasets or records available in a repository/registry/archive

44a9d6c0-f96c-47d2-8c7a-902a9c222067.text:
Imagine trying to interpret a similar data set from a colleague a few years from now.  
Is there any way in which the data could be misinterpreted? Do all researchers in the field do things in exactly the same way? Will this still be understood in10 years?  
How can misinterpretation be avoided?
44a9d6c0-f96c-47d2-8c7a-902a9c222067.title: Do you have a clear understanding of what metadata is needed to make it possible for others to read and interpret the data in the future?

44f41718-d927-41c9-930f-19fe576f6e21.text: This could be e.g. (national) security reasons, export control, protection of endangered species, non-disclosure agreements
44f41718-d927-41c9-930f-19fe576f6e21.title: Are there other legal reasons?

4539a575-6bdd-4077-a2fd-71b8121e2e06.title: Will you use a database system to store project data?

46e07a92-2070-4212-96ea-8b98da488292.title: Are you representing the graph in RDF?

4766398d-94a4-42a7-a71c-e1bccda71774.text:
Which metadata and documentation accompanies the dataset?\
Explain to the best of your knowledge what metadata standard/schema is used for your dataset. Here you may reuse information from the metadata standard question in the ‘Data documentation’ chapter.
4766398d-94a4-42a7-a71c-e1bccda71774.title: Which metadata and documentation accompanies the dataset?

49da595c-0bdf-4c97-8d28-0db5dcbe6fdb.title: How frequently are backups executed? Are snapshots created? What is the recovery strategy?

4a27da5f-f7cb-4114-8976-1caa48cb67fe.text: Ensure that the reuse conditions are sufficiently clear before you start re-using data. This will help to avoid situations where you have to redo analyses,  retract published results or enter conflicts with the original right holders or authorities.
4a27da5f-f7cb-4114-8976-1caa48cb67fe.title: Does the data/record/source have a license assigned or clear reuse conditions?

4ae6452a-4ca6-434c-8453-7e3872e41847.text: Specify an approximation of the expected data volume
4ae6452a-4ca6-434c-8453-7e3872e41847.title: Data volume in gigabytes

4baf405e-262e-4f1a-bfdc-fe72fc628650.text: Are you using any commercially licensed products for which a regular fee must be paid to be able to access and work with data, software or documents?
4baf405e-262e-4f1a-bfdc-fe72fc628650.title: Will there be software license costs or other recurring fees to work with data or documents?

4ccb2189-4235-4c65-9ed0-3a50b8d8a031.text:
Metadata is data about data, providing information needed to understand and use data both during and after a research project.

Describe how you will ensure connection between data and their respective metadata or data documentation in your project. The same strategies for linking metadata should be communicated to all project members who manages data in the project. Multiple selections will be relevant in most projects.
4ccb2189-4235-4c65-9ed0-3a50b8d8a031.title: How will you connect data and respective metadata/data documentation?

4d44346a-b8ad-4374-bf49-9d2b2cdc0685.text:
Briefly describe DMP revision plans. When are revisions of the DMP planned, e.g. at regular intervals or when a milestone in the project is reached? Specify dates, if possible.\
Will there be a routine how version numbers will be assigned (e.g. version X.Y where number X indicates a major change and number Y minor changes)?\
Who will take responsibility for revisions can be specified in the next question.

Example: The DMP will be revised before mid-term evaluation (version 2.0) of the project and when the project is ending (version 3.0). Minor revisions will be executed as needed and be assigned a version number (e.g. version 2.1).
4d44346a-b8ad-4374-bf49-9d2b2cdc0685.title: Describe revisions plans

4e71b265-092f-43fd-ac2b-4410f06f7abc.title: Yes, it can be published as open data

4e84a44f-e66b-42f5-bb42-7e6c4bd3b79b.title: Will data be collected in digital form?

4ebd52a2-4a7a-4204-9309-a4b17a4a89f0.text: Click 'Add' to add a new dataset or record.
4ebd52a2-4a7a-4204-9309-a4b17a4a89f0.title: List published/archived datasets, records or digital sources that will be (re)used in the project

4ed706a6-7ab7-47f6-9a50-531cc6847a04.title: List policies or guidelines

4ee18cdf-5dc5-41fa-b2a5-28da7d99173c.text: From which date will data be available under this license? If the data is available immediately after publication, this equals the publication date. If there is an embargo period, indicate the date the embargo ends.
4ee18cdf-5dc5-41fa-b2a5-28da7d99173c.title: Starting date when the license becomes applicable

50a17cae-451c-4867-8433-a7bf1f527ecc.title: Yes, it can be published with restricted access

50f9d580-3e62-434b-81ff-86daed56aca8.text: In some cases data has to be converted to different (persistent) file formats late in the project for archiving. Being clear about the necessary conversion steps is important.
50f9d580-3e62-434b-81ff-86daed56aca8.title: Will the data be converted to other file format(s) before archiving?

532ddc6a-3415-4df8-8fa1-002ee486fd42.text: If sources are using different semantic resources, you will need to plan time to find or make the right mappings.
532ddc6a-3415-4df8-8fa1-002ee486fd42.title: Are your sources using the same vocabularies or ontologies where possible?

535be22a-b3b6-4ab4-b9b5-8479eeb73077.text:
If relevant, describe other types of data and documents that the project will utilize or produce and which have not been captured by the previous categories. What data type/format will be used? Will specific software be needed? What documentation or metadata will accompany the data?

For some projects that handle little data, this may be the only applicable category.

Examples:
* Documents related to manuscript writing (e.g. manuscript drafts written in Word or LaTex, figures and illustrations in graphic software)
* Literature databases
* Notetaking documents
535be22a-b3b6-4ab4-b9b5-8479eeb73077.title: Other types of data the project will gather

53e90156-2047-4de1-bc28-faec0a436f2c.title: Are there different versions of this data/record?

55e8ada2-af3a-490f-8035-6f4f0be2ae04.text: Necessary arrangements to access facilities or equipment may include training project members, arranging facility access, or executing security clearance. Consider that these processes can be time-consuming.
55e8ada2-af3a-490f-8035-6f4f0be2ae04.title: Are special arrangements required to access facilities or equipment for the project?

55f03a4a-034b-422a-adf6-757416b7650a.title: Will you be allowing authenticated access to the data?

56163eb0-0101-40b4-b1c1-580875196b5c.text: Project number is a unique number assigned to the project by the authority that is undertaking the project.
56163eb0-0101-40b4-b1c1-580875196b5c.title: Project number

568ef4ca-88fe-4d82-889d-446ba5fc3da4.title: If applicable: Will you use a specific portion or subset of the data?

56e35e31-d3e2-4d50-9053-d1f66b6b03ba.text:
Where do you plan to archive your research data? Remember that this can include project results in a broad sense, which you may not immediately think of as 'data'.

What type of archive, such as using a domain-specific or institutional archive, is appropriate for your data? Archive choice often decides over necessary documentation and metadata standards to be applied, therefore it is recommended to investigate suitable archives early in the project.

Use the 'Add' button to list relevant archive(s) and respective metadata standards.
56e35e31-d3e2-4d50-9053-d1f66b6b03ba.title: Identify archive(s) for depositing datasets

577b148b-3d84-4f9c-8c53-bf6ef0d02cbe.title: How frequently are backups executed? Are snapshots created? What is the recovery strategy?

5acf079c-4f38-430b-95ae-b66041b64a60.text: You should already have identified suitable archives in the ‘Identify archive(s)’ question above. Thus, you may reuse the information you entered there.
5acf079c-4f38-430b-95ae-b66041b64a60.title: Where has the data been archived?

5b23a800-8698-497a-8ad8-7a90aef81f85.text:
Provide information about data type and file format(s).

Example:
* Measurement results in tabular format (.csv)
* Measurement results in proprietary format
* Image files in open (.tif) or proprietary format
5b23a800-8698-497a-8ad8-7a90aef81f85.title: Data format(s)

5b766af0-99ac-42fd-ad27-4c3b5b26b584.title: Please provide the URL for the domain-specific registry entry

5c22cf59-89e3-43a1-af10-1af43a97bcb2.title: What type of identifier?

5d40d071-9a87-4e76-bee5-4b9e920fdc03.text: Indicate the information security level(s) this storage solution will be used for. Several choices are possible.
5d40d071-9a87-4e76-bee5-4b9e920fdc03.title: Information security level(s) this storage solution will be used for

5d782124-f0df-4817-bfaf-6f02f143e0cb.text: Briefly describe the application procedure, requirements, and expected time frame to receive an answer.
5d782124-f0df-4817-bfaf-6f02f143e0cb.title: Describe the application procedure

5db6bd58-5d1d-4d02-9497-972cbef64c16.text: Making use of the same pseudonymization for different studies makes it possible to integrate information later. Obviously it also raises the risk of re-identification.
5db6bd58-5d1d-4d02-9497-972cbef64c16.title: Can you make use of an existing 'trusted third party' for pseudonymization?

5f329fad-bfcc-4d08-baad-46b79b4e7ac4.title: How frequently are backups executed? Who is responsible? What is the recovery strategy?

5f6a632d-f286-48e7-82e0-d498e932def1.text: Describe all software, code, computational models or simulations that will be generated during the research project. Research software, computational models and simulations are related to research data as a research output, yet more dynamic.
5f6a632d-f286-48e7-82e0-d498e932def1.title: The project will generate research software, code, computational models or simulations

614ab69d-55a6-4214-b384-00ba21ce92a1.text: There are many factors that can contribute to the risk of information loss or information leaks. They are often part of the behavior of the people that are involved in the project, but can also be steered by properly planned infrastructure.
614ab69d-55a6-4214-b384-00ba21ce92a1.title: IT security: If data is leaked, what will be the consequences?

6226d7f2-565f-4991-94b3-c00be6aca20e.text: A metadata standard/schema is a predefined set of attributes to describe data in a clear and consistent way. Metadata standards/schemas can be generic or discipline-specific, and research communities have worked together to define what type of metadata are needed when research of a certain kind is performed and described. Complete metadata helps to organise data during the project, and is necessary for data archiving.
6226d7f2-565f-4991-94b3-c00be6aca20e.title: Do suitable metadata standards/schemas exist for the data?

624cfadf-6177-47b7-9ba8-b80b68aef0fd.title: Rough average size of each file/subject in gigabytes

6295a55d-48d7-4f3c-961a-45b38eeea41f.title: ORCID Identifier

633fc867-f71e-4ee4-a203-048a010be5c7.text: Did you use any specific methodology to identify the selected dataset, record or digital source?
633fc867-f71e-4ee4-a203-048a010be5c7.title: If applicable: How was the data selected?

63b6e695-2371-412c-bed3-f13e157da0b4.text:
Are there any restrictions on how these data can be used? Describing data use permissions in a formalised way, e.g. by using the data use ontology (DUO), can greatly improves data reusability.  
Data licenses are addressed in the next question.

Examples of use restrictions:

- Reuse is permitted only for research on certain types of diseases
- Sharing is permitted only within specific geographical boundaries
63b6e695-2371-412c-bed3-f13e157da0b4.title: Are there any use restrictions for these data?

63f0095f-5b22-46c8-ba10-da6c6ffc5831.text: Indicate the single posts and/or the expected sum. If you are converting currencies, remember to indicate the conversion rate.
63f0095f-5b22-46c8-ba10-da6c6ffc5831.title: If applicable: Related costs

640ef431-69dd-40fa-90e1-409ec507a5ff.text: Enter the url under which the dataset can be accessed.
640ef431-69dd-40fa-90e1-409ec507a5ff.title: Persistent identifier (PID) of the dataset

65730a4d-40db-46c9-b6d2-2b44b35f617e.text: Are you using e.g. an Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) or Electronic Data Capture (EDC) application? Such applications often have their own data structure that can only be accessed through the application.
65730a4d-40db-46c9-b6d2-2b44b35f617e.title: Are you storing (some of your) data in an application specific manner?

657f768b-8bba-49b4-ae09-86e45a0cc295.text: If relevant for these data or deviating from the information provided in chapter 'Storage and backup', please describe considerations regarding information security, storage, backup, data protection.

68530470-1f1c-4448-8593-63a288713a66.text:

In case the organisation is not listed, preferably enter the url to its homepage and click outside of the text box to save the string.
68530470-1f1c-4448-8593-63a288713a66.title: Affiliation

69be6695-152b-48ba-a1fd-6662476e39b7.text: Aggregated data, where typically at least 15 individuals are in any data point, are considered sufficiently anonymous. This is an alternative way of making data openly available for future research.
69be6695-152b-48ba-a1fd-6662476e39b7.title: Could you use data aggregation to make the data openly available?

6ab36a2a-23ca-484d-b3d7-0c1a4eea821a.text: A label like 'temperature' only makes sense to a computer if it is also clear what the units are and what temperature has been measured. In many cases, it is also important how it was measured.
6ab36a2a-23ca-484d-b3d7-0c1a4eea821a.title: Do all columns/headers have a data type?

6bfde30e-90e7-4422-b984-d571196194e0.text: In a table, the data items are arranged in columns. Is there a header for each of these describing what is in there?
6bfde30e-90e7-4422-b984-d571196194e0.title: Does each column have a header?

6fd5c7ea-2240-411b-8110-3f7b5abb797e.text:
If data(sets) are or will be deleted, they can be listed in this question, including a brief description of the reasons for deletion.

Use 'Add' button for any new dataset.
6fd5c7ea-2240-411b-8110-3f7b5abb797e.title: List data that you have deleted or will delete

7004da20-a0ec-46a6-b3c4-eca87686e4de.text: Briefly describe the storage solution. If applicable, you can link to a description of the storage solution provided by your IT department or other provider.

72099c46-16e7-47a2-a320-cc768b7085fe.text:
Will you be using a working space containing data and software specific to the project that is shared between all the people working on the data in the project? Sometimes such a system is called a *Virtual Research Environment*. 

You may find this partially overlapping with the chapter 'Storing and protecting data during the project', yet emphasis here is put on data processing.
72099c46-16e7-47a2-a320-cc768b7085fe.title: Do you need a shared space with your collaborators to work on data analysis?

72e07a9b-8a61-4b3b-b4cb-686ad4cbe9fe.title: Name of the archive/repository where the dataset(s) are published

73d686bd-7939-412e-8631-502ee6d9ea7b.text:
List all persons contributing to the data management plan and/or data management in the research project. Use the 'Add' button for adding a new person.

All projects should at least have a Contact Person and a Project Leader. A person can have several roles. In educational settings, the supervisor(s) should be specified as they have formal responsibility for data management.
73d686bd-7939-412e-8631-502ee6d9ea7b.title: Contributor(s)

Search by Norwegian or English organisation name to see a list of suggestions from the Research Organisation Registry (ROR).\
In case the organisation is not listed, preferably enter the url to its homepage and click outside of the text box to save the string.
76a2e7e3-4f65-4366-b86c-1347ccc9ba5c.title: Institution/partner

77b971e5-a649-4a64-af0d-5ddcf548a6fa.text: Briefly describe the dataset or source you are planning to make use of. What data type(s) and formats does the dataset consist of? What is the data volume?

7ae72f9e-a694-436f-b605-269357016a16.title: List contributors who will be responsible for data management

Begin typing to see a list of suggestions based on structured information in the FAIRsharing registry.

NB! Return to manual entry if you cannot find the data format/type.
7d0ddb9b-8c02-420b-ba25-0a4b11fbac6b.title: Data format

7d4e1a7d-bab4-4eed-87a1-8c50699f0f30.text: It is recommended to publish the DMP with a persistent identifier in a research repository that supports versioning, for example [zenodo](https://zenodo.org/). Ideally all iterations will be published/archived, but at minimum the final version.
7d4e1a7d-bab4-4eed-87a1-8c50699f0f30.title: Have you published, or will you publish (versions of) this DMP?

7dcf6059-b22c-44e9-8048-c8b8b90764ca.text: Make sure names are relatively short, and avoid spaces and special characters. You can use underscore characters, and consider using unique identifiers for the samples/experiments, workflow invocations, and processing steps. You can consider to add versioning using the date in YYYYMMDD format.
7dcf6059-b22c-44e9-8048-c8b8b90764ca.title: What are the naming conventions for objects in the store?

7ed9939b-b85c-48bf-87f5-2aa081bb5267.text:
A title following the scheme "Data Management Plan for: Project identificator" is recommended.

Example:
* If your research project's name is "A really fantastic project" and you use the acronym AMAZING, the DMP title should be "Data Management Plan for AMAZING - A really fantastic project, 2020-2024"
7ed9939b-b85c-48bf-87f5-2aa081bb5267.title: DMP title

8018e15f-6c7e-433a-8686-ae0d695c7fc7.title: Does the domain specific format come with its own suite of integration tools that you will use?

811965f0-b1b7-4b4b-92a6-01cd83b08fa2.title: Are the data sources using linked data?

81d3095e-a530-40a4-878e-ced42fabc4cd.text:
List both datasets that are or will be made available by depositing them in a research data archive openly or with restricted access, and datasets that you are planning to preserve but not make accessible to others. Think about a dataset as some collection of data that will end up in the same place.

Use 'Add' button for any new dataset.
81d3095e-a530-40a4-878e-ced42fabc4cd.title: List datasets that you have archived or will archive

Search by dataset name, author or DOI (in the format 10.1000/xyz123, without the prefix) to see a list of suggestions from the DataCite registry.

NB! Only applicable to datasets with a DOI, issued/indexed by DataCite. Return to manual entry if you cannot find the dataset.
81ee2b89-b3b0-4628-b7ff-dcff443595ed.title: DOI search

81f18ab3-ab64-46a7-bf26-9e7d663d4362.title: What is the basis for the restrictions?

829dcda6-db8a-40ac-819a-92b9b52490f5.text:
Indicate all roles that apply for the contributor, several choices are possible. You should specify at least one "Contact Person". If your project has a work package for data management, identify the leader of that work package as "Data Steward".

<!-- LL 15.8.24: Burde man ikke få frem,  i Contributor - role, hvem som har (redigerings-)adgang til dataene i aktiv fase? Hvilke roller har denne adgangen? -->

8316d92f-30c5-4cd6-9e9f-7aa0143f79b9.title: If applicable: Document number

83c0d09d-e74c-4c81-a52c-aaa2e18415ac.text:
In the context of research data management this could include:
* Data Stewards
* Data Engineers
* Software developers
* Data entry specialists
* Data protection specialists
* Legal advisers
83c0d09d-e74c-4c81-a52c-aaa2e18415ac.title: To execute the project, is additional expertise required?

84add0b3-2828-4c20-b741-3d7136ef655e.text: Briefly describe the dataset. Include data type, estimated volume, and origin of the dataset. You can refer to the information provided in the chapters 'Reusing data', 'Creating and collecting data' and 'Processing and analyzing data'.

84ec46bd-e714-4d98-8525-cdd0d58777ef.text: If using cloud services always choose options in line with institutional storage guidelines, and preferably with institutional log-on.

948b5fd8-c1bd-457d-8f81-ea2fd093f541.text: To make data understandable, reproducible and reusable, it is crucial that the data origin and relevant parameters as well as all processing and filtering steps are documented. This is called data provenance or data lineage.

For each collaboration, you can also specify related contracts/collaboration agreements.

Describe your file naming strategy. It is recommended to agree on a file naming scheme that everyone in the project will be following.

Make sure names are relatively short, and avoid spaces and special characters other than underscores.
You can consider to include unique identifiers e.g. for experiments. Versioning by date should follow the YYYYMMDD format.

a18d79d2-74b1-4524-a3c7-2c1a87b66a75.title: Are there intellectual property (IP) reasons why your data cannot be open?

a1d76760-053c-4706-80a2-cfb6c6a061f3.text:
Is there anything in this dataset that could be tied to a person? This could be a physical characteristic, but also behavior of a person, movements, communications. Note that e.g. readouts about the performance of an airplane are considered to contain personal data of the pilot!

The GDPR states that the processing (including storing) of personal data should stop when the intended purpose of the processing is done. There are, however, exemptions to this e.g. when the processing is done for research purposes. Also, from a research ethics point of view, research data should be kept to make it possible for others to validate published research findings and reuse data for new discoveries. This is also governed by what the data subjects have been informed about regarding how you will treat the data after project completion. The recommendation is to deposit the sensitive data in the appropriate controlled access repositories if such are available, but this requires that the data subjects are informed and have agreed to this.
a1d76760-053c-4706-80a2-cfb6c6a061f3.title: Does this dataset contain personal data?

a1f9f1a1-dd08-488b-abbd-a232e1845ee5.title: Which legal basis for processing of personal data applies?

a205f751-cbb6-4f58-a692-2ba450e1e133.title: How will the data be digitalised?

a20ddc5d-e883-4bc4-9948-36a2cb477d10.text: Your institute may have a generic risk assessment; you should be informed about this.
a20ddc5d-e883-4bc4-9948-36a2cb477d10.title: Did you consider the possible impact to the project or organization if information leaks?

a25b30f4-2d0f-4132-9b8e-0950f0b0ed66.text: Legally, pseudonymous data (which means that someone has the key to reverse the process) is still considered privacy sensitive information. However, the EU is working on special cases where the data can still be opened as long as the key availability is sufficiently limited.
a25b30f4-2d0f-4132-9b8e-0950f0b0ed66.title: Could pseudonymization be used to make the data more openly available?

a2b1fa38-792a-4628-9765-93476a38cffb.text: Note: Be aware that different laws might define the mandatory content of an informed consent in different ways. Therefore, an informed consent that is valid under one law (e.g. ethical legislation), might not be valid under another law (e.g. GDPR). Informed consents acquired before the GDPR might not be valid if consent is to be used as the legal basis for processing.
a2b1fa38-792a-4628-9765-93476a38cffb.title: Have informed consents been collected from the research subjects?

a2e22a59-4747-4c29-84c0-3a9cbd859249.text: Keeping track of file versions can be important part of the data lineage (provenance). Will you define rules for file versioning? Will you use a version control system such as git?
a2e22a59-4747-4c29-84c0-3a9cbd859249.title: Will you handle different versions of files or documents?

ae28a862-5020-44c2-8c78-3abc185b190f.text: It is not always clear to everyone in the project (or outside it) what can and can not be done with a dataset. It is therefore helpful to associate each dataset with a license as early as possible in the project. The license should be machine-readable wherever possible. A data license should ideally be as little restrictive as possible.

bdaba73c-02e0-4ff1-acb3-a68df891c36a.text: For a good understanding of tabular data, you need to make the relationship between each pair of columns explicit. E.g. if one column is 'disease' and another is 'treatment', you want to make sure that this is the chosen treatment that this person is undergoing for the given disease.
bdaba73c-02e0-4ff1-acb3-a68df891c36a.title: Are all the relations between the column headers explicit?

c47d330a-2eb4-472e-be48-d06b3ec9b8e8.text: If there are reasonable limitations to the values in a column, or even a limited set of allowed values, it is very good for data validation and reusability if these limitations are explicit, and e.g. software used for data entry and editing will not allow anything else.
c47d330a-2eb4-472e-be48-d06b3ec9b8e8.title: Are the limitations to allowed data values in each column explicit?

c520b63c-cc35-4789-9d98-cc87b59ebcf1.text: How does the project in this DMP relate to other projects when it comes to data? Who could be interested in making use of the data? This question relates both to general reuse possibilities for these type of data and concrete plans to reuse the data from this project. Several choices are possible.
c520b63c-cc35-4789-9d98-cc87b59ebcf1.title: Who else could be interested in using data from this project?

c719e371-f1de-468d-b116-5c12a5bdf3dc.title: Description of the dataset
